perturbation.original_text: The Disclosee agree s that the Seller s or Advisors shall not have any liability to the Disclosee or to any of their directors, employees, advisors or agents resulting from the use of Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Disclosee agree s that the Seller s or Advisors shall have no liability to the Disclosee or to any of their directors, employees, advisors or agents resulting from the use of Confidential Information, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original intention of limiting liability. Originally, the Sellers and Advisors had no liability related to the Confidential Information. Adding 'except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct' now introduces potential liability, contradicting the prior statement. This creates a contradiction within the contract because there is uncertainty regarding the obligations to not be held liable for actions related to Confidential Information and there is now the potentiality to be held liable. This affects the section that allows for injuctive relief
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of an actual or threatened breach by the Disclosee and/or its Representatives of any provisions of this Agreement , the Sellers shall be entitled (i) t o seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction restraining the Disclosee and/or its Representatives from breaching the terms hereof or from disclosing any Confidential Information to any person and (ii) specific performance and other equit able relief.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the Sellers and Advisors had no liability related to the Confidential Information. The changed text introduces an exception to this, stating that they can be held liable in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct. This directly contradicts the original intention of limiting liability, which creates uncertainty regarding the obligations to not be held liable. The contradicted text allows the Sellers to seek injunctive relief in the event of a breach by the Disclosee. Because the Changed Text introduces the possibility of liability, the injunctive relief sought could be impacted, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of the Disclosee under this Agreement shall expire the earlier of (a) t hree years after the date of this Agreement, or (b) the date the Sale between the Candidate and the Sell ers is completed.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of the Disclosee under this Agreement shall expire the earlier of (a) t hree years after the date of this Agreement, or (b) the date the Sale between the Candidate and the Sell ers is completed, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly defines the expiration of the Disclosee's obligations. Adding 'unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties' introduces an exception that contradicts the defined expiration. The expiration is no longer certain, creating uncertainty about the duration of the confidentiality obligations. This change contradicts the section that states obligations of confidentiality and non-disclosure shall become effective as of the date of signing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations of confidentiality and non -disclosure under this Agreement shall become effective as of the date of signing. In the event that the Disclosee or any of its Representatives becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Confidential Information to a regulatory authority or to any other entity or third party, the Disclosee shall immediately notify the Sellers before disclosing such Confidential In formation, so that the Sellers may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, without prejudice to t he Disclosee’s remedies under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states a clear expiration condition. The changed text introduces an exception ('unless otherwise agreed upon in writing'), which allows for the obligations to continue beyond the initially defined expiration, directly contradicting the statement that the obligations 'shall expire' at a defined point. This directly impacts the contradicted text because the effective date of signing and subsequent confidentiality obligations are now uncertain beyond the initial expiration term. It introduces an exception that wasn't there originally.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with Belgian law and the Parties irrevocably agree to submit themselves to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of Brussels.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with Belgian law and the Parties irrevocably agree to submit themselves to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of Brussels, except in cases where arbitration is mutually agreed upon.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies exclusive jurisdiction in the Courts of Brussels. Adding 'except in cases where arbitration is mutually agreed upon' contradicts this by introducing an alternative dispute resolution method. It creates uncertainty about where disputes will be resolved, contradicting the 'irrevocably agree to submit' clause. It directly contradicts the last sentence which shows the acceptance of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance wit h the procedure defined in the c andidacy file, w e would be grateful if you could confirm your understanding and acceptance of the foregoing by returning to us a signed copy of the present document Yours sincerely, _______________ _______________ Frédéric Sohet Koen Van Gerven Partner CEO Deloitte Real Estate | Financial Advisory * bpost SA/NV The Candidate / Disclosee Name . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . Company . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . Title . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . Date . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . Signature
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies exclusive jurisdiction in the Courts of Brussels. The changed text adds an exception allowing for arbitration if mutually agreed upon. The contradicted text requires the party to confirm their understanding and acceptance of the agreement by signing a copy. Since the changed text introduces a potential alternative to the jurisdiction, it contradicts the acceptance by creating an alternative to the exclusive jurisdiction, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction by implying the acceptance is conditional.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall exclude: a) information which had become available to the public prior to the date of disclosure thereof to the Disclosee or their Representatives, or that became available to the public after such date other than as a result of a breach of this Agreement by the Disclosee or their Representatives; b) information which was lawfully in the Disclosee ’s possession or in the possess ion of one or more of their Representatives prior to disclosure under this Agreement, in each case on a non -confidential basis, which, to their knowledge was free of any restriction as to its use or disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information shall exclude: a) information which had become available to the public prior to the date of disclosure thereof to the Disclosee or their Representatives, or that became available to the public after such date other than as a result of a breach of this Agreement by the Disclosee or their Representatives; b) information which was lawfully in the Disclosee ’s possession or in the possess ion of one or more of their Representatives prior to disclosure under this Agreement, in each case on a non -confidential basis, which, to their knowledge was free of any restriction as to its use or disclosure. However, the Sellers reserve the right to retroactively classify publicly available information as Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This modification directly contradicts the established definition of excluded information. While initially excluding publicly available information, the addition of 'However, the Sellers reserve the right to retroactively classify publicly available information as Confidential Information' negates the exclusion. This creates direct conflict and uncertainty about what information is truly excluded from confidentiality, and creates a contradiction with the part of the same section where the "Disclosee acknowledge s that they may not approach the representatives of the Sellers".
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Disclosee acknowledge s that they may not approach the representatives of the Sellers and cannot contact anybody else other than the contact persons from the Advisors . The Disclosee further undertake s and acknowledge s, that neither the representatives of the Sellers nor their Advisors give any warranty or make any representation as to the accuracy or completeness of any of the Confidential Information or as to the reasonableness of any assumptions on which the information is based.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause allowing Sellers to retroactively classify publicly available information as confidential, which directly contradicts the original definition of 'Confidential Information' that excludes publicly available information. This creates uncertainty and directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' clause that states 'The Disclosee acknowledge s that they may not approach the representatives of the Sellers'. This stems from the logic that if the information that the disclosee would use to approach the sellers is deemed confidential retroactively, then it would be considered a breach. This is a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition. The contradicted text relies on a consistent definition of confidential information, which the changed text undermines.

perturbation.original_text: The Disclosee further undertake s and acknowledge s, that neither the representatives of the Sellers nor their Advisors give any warranty or make any representation as to the accuracy or completeness of any of the Confidential Information or as to the reasonableness of any assumptions on which the information is based.
perturbation.changed_text: The Disclosee further undertake s and acknowledge s, that the representatives of the Sellers and their Advisors warrant the Confidential Information is accurate, complete, and based on reasonable assumptions. However, the Sellers retain the right to later claim inaccuracies or incompleteness.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction by stating that the representatives of the Sellers do warrant the information, which directly opposes the initial statement that they do not. The statement 'However, the Sellers retain the right to later claim inaccuracies or incompleteness' further amplifies the contradiction because it introduces a potential for future denial of the warrant. This change affects the first paragraph where it states 'In consideration of the Sellers agreeing to disclose confidential information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality Agreement This Confidentiality Agreement (the “ Agreement ”) has been prepared on behalf of bpost SA/NV, a limited liability company of public law, and the C ity of Brussels (the “Sellers ”) with regard to the divestment of their respective surface areas (the “ Sale”) in the Munt Center /Centre Monnaie (the “Munt ”). In consideration of the Sellers agreeing to disclose confidential information (the “ Confidential Information ”) to the Candidate (“Disclosee ”), the latter hereby undertakes to the Sellers that as Disclosee , they will keep confidential all and any Confidential Information received at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the representatives of the Sellers do not give any warranty as to the accuracy of the confidential information. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that the representatives warrant the information is accurate. This directly contradicts the consideration in the 'Confidentiality Agreement' where the 'Sellers agreeing to disclose confidential information' is in exchange for the 'Disclosee' keeping the information confidential. Because the warranties of the confidential information are directly conflicting, the consideration is affected.
